title: Workflow Syntax, Expressions, YAML, and Data Contracts · Release Evidence Brief: Ci Workflow Schema Top Level
summary: A workflow parses but triggers unexpectedly, treats false as text, expands a multiline output incorrectly, inherits a permissive default, and hides a misspelled key. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci workflow schema top level increment in validated workflow data contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Author name, on, permissions, concurrency, env, defaults, jobs, and job-level contracts with explicit supported syntax.
Use repository, workflow ref, workflow SHA, run ID, run attempt, actor, triggering actor, event, ref, and SHA to identify exact execution evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example workflow parses YAML but treats false as text, expands a multiline value unsafely, uses an unavailable context, and accepts a misspelled input. Validate syntax, types, timing, shell boundaries, and a changed event fixture. Competency probe: Author name, on, permissions, concurrency, env, defaults, jobs, and job-level contracts with explicit supported syntax. A defensible solution applies Ci Workflow Schema Top Level, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to validated workflow data contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-workflow-schema-top-level — Author name, on, permissions, concurrency, env, defaults, jobs, and job-level contracts with explicit supported syntax.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-gha-workflow-syntax-expressions-4
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-workflow-syntax-expressions-4
    artifact: workflow-contract.yaml
    decision: worked gha workflow syntax expressions 4
    environment: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runner on a cold cache
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      name: verified-ci
      on-events: [pull_request, push]
      permissions: contents-read
      concurrency: revision-scoped
      expression-input: parsed-with-fromJSON
      shell-input: validated-environment
      schema: action-inputs-reviewed
      changed-event-fixture: fork-pull-request
    verify: validate workflow schema expressions context availability action inputs and changed event fixtures
    failure: Reject valid-YAML-only review, implicit coercion, unavailable contexts, or direct untrusted expression interpolation.
    repair: Validate workflow semantics, parse explicit types, move untrusted values through data boundaries, and test changed payloads.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
